missions.name: Air Show!
missions.objective: Acquire Air Show Entrance Badge 0.1 m3
missions.briefing: Congratulation! You've been invited to participate in the   joint air show. You just have to show off your piloting skills with some fancy maneuvers. Nothing you can't handle. Oh, you need to go pick up your Entrance Badge.

missions.name: Alluring Emanations
missions.objective: Investigate the asteroid belt. Retreat if you encounter overwhelming force.
missions.briefing:
Yes, I certainly do have something for you. Recently, some explorers employed by Nugoeihuvi Corporation discovered an extremely strong gravimetric signature. Their scanners indicated the hidden belt was full of rare ore. Now, while this news would normally be cause for joy, I'm suspicious.

I'd like you to fly over to the belt and check it out. If it really is full of ore-- wonderful. If not, get back to me with the news as quickly as possible.

missions.name: An Ancient Roster
missions.objective: Retrieve the Brutor Tribe Roster (0.2) from the jettisoned cargo container.
missions.briefing:
You look pretty green to me, youngster. But we've got a situation here and maybe you can help us out. 

The frigate Sunhammer was recently attacked by Angel Cartel pirates; the captain called for reinforcements and managed to warp away, but the Angels scanned him down and pursued him, and the ship was lost... yet not before the fast-thinking captain jettisoned his cargo to avoid the risk of its being destroyed in the firefight. The captain's pod made it back in one piece, and the cargo's mostly expendible , but for one thing: Among the junk they had to ditch was a Brutor Tribe roster with all
missions.blitz: Approach the container, get the Roster and warp out.

missions.name: An End to Eavesdropping
missions.objective: Destroy the listening post and any ships defending it.
missions.briefing:
Our counter-intelligence service has indicated that the Gallente Federal Intelligence Office has erected a covert listening post in . While I assure you our communications are encrypted, the presence of Gallente intelligence operations in our space poses both an insult to Caldari sovereignty and an unacceptable risk to communications security.

Your task is simple: go there, overwhelm whatever forces are defending the post, and destroy it. Can I count on you to see this through?

missions.name: Athran Exigency
missions.objective: Approach Abandoned Mining Station
missions.briefing:
You don't say! Well, yes, in fact, I do have something I could use you for. 

You've heard of the "Athran Brotherhood"? No? Well, that's no surprise, really. They're little more than a nuisance, and few in number. But they can be a pain at times. And we're pretty sure there's a chapter of them hereabouts. But we don't know where they're hiding out. That's what I want you to find out for me, actually. 

There's a small station built into an old asteroid near here. Used to be a mining outpost till they hollowed the thing out. It's been abandoned for a few years now, but some rumors of activity i

pockets.name: 20-30km, spawn when you are within 10km of the station

npcs.name: Cosensin Thrand
npcs.notes: Has a longer range than the other ships

missions.blitz: Destroy the Amarr Starbase Control Tower, leaving the ships alone.

missions.name: Avenge a Fallen Comrade (Angel Cartel)
missions.objective: Destroy the habitat of the pirate leaders then report back to your agent.
missions.briefing:
Today is a sad day, . One of our freelancers, working on a simple reconnaissance job in , was brutally murdered by Angel Cartel thugs while on duty. He was well liked in , and we will miss his loyal service to us greatly. 

Now, you might be thinking, "How does this affect me?" Well I'll tell you. We aren't letting this outrage go unpunished. I want the people behind this brutal murder taken care of immediately. I will supply you with a bookmark to their hideout, which is heavily guarded by Cartel fighters. Head out there and avenge your colleague by destroying their habitation module; it's th
missions.blitz: Destroy Habitat, warp out.

missions.objective: Destroy the habitat of the pirate leaders then report back to your agent.

I will supply you with a bookmark to their hideout, which is heavily guarded by Raiders. Head out there and avenge your colleague by destroying their habitation module; it's the primar
missions.blitz: Destroy Habitat, warp out.

missions.name: Avenge a Fallen Comrade (DED)
missions.objective: Destroy all enemy ships

missions.name: Avenge a Fallen Comrade (Guristas Pirates)
missions.objective: Destroy the Habitat
missions.blitz: Destroy the Habitat, warp out.

missions.objective: Destroy the habitat of the pirate leaders then report back to your agent.
missions.briefing:
Today is a sad day, . One of our freelancers, working on a simple reconnaissance job in , was brutally murdered by Serpentis thugs while on duty. He was well liked in ., and we will miss his loyal service to us greatly. 

Now, you might be thinking, "How does this affect me?" Well I'll tell you. We aren't letting this outrage go unpunished. I want the people behind this brutal murder taken care of immediately. I will supply you with a bookmark to their hideout, which is heavily guarded by Serpentis fighters. Head out there and avenge your colleague by destroying their habitation module; it's t
missions.blitz: No hostiles at initial gate.

missions.name: Break Their Will (Angel Cartel)
missions.objective: Find the repair station at the location your agent gives you, and attack and destroy it.
missions.briefing:
We've discovered a small repair station in . By itself, this station is no issue, but when our patrols scanned the site they detected pirates in the area. This particular repair station is owned by a local mercenary gang that allows felonious individuals to dock there, and they are therefore aiding and abetting known criminals. 

I would like you to do us all a favor and remove this repair station from existence. It's not registered in the government's official database and it's being used to break the law ― that is all the information I need to authorize decisive action against it. It may be
missions.blitz: Destroy Repair Station, warp out.

missions.name: Cargo Delivery (Angel Cartel)
missions.objective: Retrieve the cargo from the warehouse, and bring it to your agent.
missions.briefing:
Here is a mission suited for someone of your caliber.

I need a shipment of Synthetic Oil delivered to this station as soon as possible. It's currently stored in one of our warehouses in ; I'll give you the coordinates, of course. Just warp to our outpost, navigate to the warehouse, and pick up the shipment, and then return it to . Simple, ain't it? 

Just make sure you keep a lookout for any suspicious activity. You can't be too careful these days.

npcs.name: Gistii Ambusher/Raider
npcs.trigger: Destroying will spawn the second wave
npcs.notes: There is only one ship of trigger type in the wave

missions.blitz: Retrieve the cargo from the warehouse, and bring it to your agent.

missions.name: Cargo Delivery (Blood Raiders)
missions.objective: Retrieve the goods (60m3)
missions.blitz: Warp-in, grab the goods, warp out.

missions.name: Carrying AIMEDs
missions.objective: Pick up the AIMEDs (25) and return them to your agent.
missions.briefing:
Good. Listen, some idiot just hacked into the AID system and fried all the doctors. Then a bigger idiot burned out all the replacement hardware by trying to replace single units instead of rebooting the whole damn system. So the whole station is fracked, completely without medical staff. 

I need you to go pick up the nearest batch of replacement parts from the Sisters of EVE Medical Center in  and bring them back here before everyone manages to die of the Debylian flu or something.

npcs.group: Group 2 (20 km) after a short delay
npcs.name: Mercenary Thief

missions.name: Clear the Traderoute
missions.objective: Destroy all enemy ships

missions.name: Closing the Gate
missions.objective: Destroy the smuggler stargate

missions.name: CONCORD Scout Spotted
missions.briefing:
A CONCORD scout drone was just spotted in this system by one of our men. As usual, these drones should always be eliminated before they cause any damage. I'm not sure why this lone drone has entered our territory. Might just be an accident, or maybe it's on some secret mission. 

Either way, I'd like you to head out there and get rid of it before it manages to escape. Leave no trace of it behind. Don't be long!

missions.name: Corporate Records
missions.briefing:
You look pretty green to me, youngster. Still, we've got a situation here and maybe you can help us out. 

Our freighter Bright Prospect struck an asteroid when emerging from warp in Isikano. The jump drive was badly damaged and they thought it might blow, but they managed to make it back in one piece. However, among the cargo, which was mostly expendable, was a set of important Ishukone corporate records; they were forced to jettison the records lest they be lost if the ship's warp core blew. At present we don't have backups due to a database malfunction, so those records might be irreplaceab
missions.blitz: Sometimes Group 1 from Pocket 2 spawns in Pocket 1 instead.

missions.name: Denial Of Reconnaissance
missions.objective: Wait for a Gallente recon ship and then destroy it.
missions.briefing: Head for your assigned patrol sector coordinates. Wait there and destroy any enemy ships that show up. Make sure none of them escape!

missions.name: Desperate Measures
missions.objective: Destroy the Subspace Data Miner
missions.briefing: Recent reports from the  give us reason to believe that the UDI terrorist organization has begun setting up outposts in the names of front corporations at deadspace forward points within our sovereign space, with the intention of spying on  corporations. I've just gotten word that there's one such suspected outpost in this system. The problem is that we cannot be fully sure whether the outpost in question is indeed a UDI hive. Obviously this means we can't go in flying our own banner, so non-contracted free agents such as yourself will be of paramount importance in the staging of preliminary o

npcs.name: Subspace Data Miner
npcs.trigger: Destroy it to complete mission objectives

missions.blitz: Destroy Subspace Data Miner

missions.name: Drone Detritus
missions.objective: Defeat rogue drones; bring a rogue drone AI core to your agent.
missions.briefing:
I've been hearing about an abandoned drone hive nearby. We have yet to find any viable explanation for its presence. I need someone to go investigate and make sure there aren't any rogue drones still hanging around: We need to be certain this is not a threat.
|collapsed= yes

missions.name: Eliminate a Pirate Nuisance (Angel Cartel)
missions.objective: Eliminate the pirates causing the nuisance
missions.briefing: A couple rogue pirates have been harassing our corporation members in . They are nothing more than a nuisance and have yet to do any real damage, other than delay our members in their travels while they chase them off. I will supply you with a bookmark to their suspected hideout. It's located nearby, so this should be a relatively straight forward mission. I will give you  ISK for completing the task, and if you're fast I'll even throw in a bonus.

missions.objective: Eliminate the pirates causing the nuisance

missions.name: Eliminate the Pirate Campers (Sansha's Nation)
missions.objective: Eliminate the Sansha ships roaming around the stargate near the coordinates of your bookmark.
missions.briefing:
I have a job for someone of your caliber. There's a hidden stargate in  that is frequented by pilots of the Amarr Empire. It so happens that a gang of vagabonds, belonging to the Sansha's Nation, have set up camp near the stargate in the hopes of catching easy prey traveling via the gate. In other words, they will attack any passersby who aren't part of their terrorist organization. 

We've considered sending in the military, but I'm afraid the pirates might make a quick get-away should they discover who's after them. I'd like you to go there and waste them once they attack you. When you've de

missions.name: Eliminate the Pirate Campers (Serpentis)
missions.objective: Eliminate the Serpentis ships roaming around the stargate
missions.briefing: There's a hidden stargate in  that is frequented by pilots of the Minmatar Republic. It so happens that a gang of Serpentis cretins has set up camp near the stargate in the hopes of catching easy prey traveling via the gate. We've considered sending in the military, but I'm afraid the pirates might make a quick get-away should they discover who's after them. I'd like you to go there and waste them once they attack you, and then return to me for your reward.

missions.name: Encounter at Station 464
missions.objective: Go to Relay Station 464, hack into the relay station and bring the Data Chip within to your agent.
missions.briefing:
, the Caldari have built an observation base in Gallente space. It has been designated “Station 464.” One of the structures is a relay station, and it is being used to store all the information the Caldari have collected in the system. I need you to hack the relay station so we can see what kind of information they have collected and thus the purpose of the base. 

The data center is heavily protected so be ready for a real fight. I will upload the location to your Neocom.
missions.blitz: Hack the relay station. Pick up the Data Chip and warp out.

missions.name: Endless Battle
missions.objective: Bring 1x Stolen Documents to your agent

missions.name: Escaped Dissident
missions.objective: Recapture Parriaval Mastes and return him to agent.
missions.briefing: You've heard of Parriaval Mastes, the infamous troublemaker? Well, he's escaped from the prison camp where he was held. We need someone to locate him and bring him back here discreetly. If word of his escape gets out, political dissidents from all across the Empire will start causing trouble. Fortunately, we've tracked the ship he stole and located its signal at a suspected terrorists' outpost in . Are you willing to fly out there and retrieve him? There's a significant reward.

missions.name: Escaped Killer
missions.objective: Recapture Milare Terhellen and return him to agent.
missions.briefing: The murderer Milare Terhellen has escaped from the prison camp where he was held. We need someone to locate him and bring him back here discreetly. Fortunately, we've tracked the ship he stole and located its signal at a suspected terrorists' outpost in . Fly out there and retrieve him. There's a significant reward.
missions.blitz: Destroy Milare's frigate, pick him up out of the container and get out.

missions.name: Escaped Spy
missions.briefing: A corporate spy has escaped from the prison camp where he was held. We need someone to locate him and bring him back here discreetly. If word of his escape gets out, dissidents all across the State will start causing trouble. Fortunately, we've tracked the ship he stole and located its signal at a suspected terrorists' outpost in [system]. Are you willing to fly out there and retrieve him? There's a significant reward.

missions.name: For Justice
missions.objective: Kill the Amarrian spy and return with the Amarr sympathizer.
missions.briefing:
Excellent. Because the Republic needs your help. Twelve hours ago, an Amarrian spy attacked and destroyed a prison convoy in the  system. Most of the guards and prisoners are dead, but one prisoner was rescued: a suspected Amarr sympathizer. 

I wish I could say Amarr sympathizers were uncommon. We like to tell ourselves that we cleaned house when we expelled the Nefantar tribe – the Ammatar – from the Republic. But of course life is not so simple. For every ten Minmatar we have fighting to free our brethren from Amarrian captivity or to restore some bit of our displaced old ways, there is one

missions.name: For the Birds
missions.briefing:
The Achuran White Song bird has been sacred to the Achura throughout their recorded history. The creature mates only in the mountains of Saisio III, and naturally is exceedingly rare. Recently, the remnants of the old Achuran government made a gift of two such birds to the State, a male and a female. These particular birds are quite special, in that they have been genetically modified to mate off-planet. This is a matter of tremendous importance for us, from both a diplomatic and an ecological standpoint. 

Twelve hours ago, the birds were stolen. Gallentean eco-terrorists hijacked the diploma

npcs.name: Gallentean Terrorist
npcs.notes: Drops container with Achuran White Song Birds (0.0m3)

missions.name: For the Honor of Rouvenor
missions.objective: Kill the lead terrorist and recover the Armor of Rouvenor.
missions.briefing:
Those damned Caldari aren't content to raze our borders and sabotage our economic concerns abroad. Oh, no! Now the bastards are attempting to defile our cultural history! 

You've heard the legends of King Rouvenor, yes? Of course you have -- every Gallentean who went to primary school has. Founded the kingdom of Garoun, reigned for a hundred years of peace, killed the fabled Giant Long-Limb of Lyace? Everyone knows these tales. Well, most believe them only to be tales. However, a recent archaeological dig has found what could be the old city of Amatore itself, the wondrous capitol of King Rou

missions.name: Forbidden Cloning
missions.objective: Stop the scientist's heretical experiments: Destroy his lab and all its guardians.
missions.briefing:
Recently, rumors have come to my attention that some... scientist has taken it upon himself to attempt to clone a member of a noble Amarr family.

This heretic must not be allowed to go through with his blasphemous experiments. I need you to be the instrument of God's will in this matter.

Destroy his facility completely. Let him answer to God for his actions.

missions.name: Freedom And Commerce
missions.objective: Destroy the Slaver Ship and rescue the slaves.
missions.briefing:
Alright, then. An Amarrian Holder we've suspected for some time of black market slave trading has overstepped his bounds and nabbed a few loyal Gallente citizens for his slave pens. You must stop him. He thinks he's getting away unnoticed, but I want you to prove him wrong. 

Destroy this scumbag's ship and bring those slaves back to me, . We'll make sure they are returned to their homes. After they pay back their transport fees, that is.

npcs.name: Slaver Ship
npcs.notes: On destruction Freed Slaves appear in cargo hold

missions.name: Furrier Fiasco
missions.objective: Destroy the infested laboratory.
missions.briefing:
It seems that some rogue scientists just could not leave Creation well enough alone. One man in particular, one "Vilus Seermal," has apparently been performing genetic experiments on the fuzzy little critters, in a hidden laboratory not far from here. While this is not illegal, the ethics of his experiments are certainly in question. He has, for whatever reason, apparently turned the things into cute, cuddly killing machines. 

Well, we just received an emergency transmission from the laboratory. Listen. 

"We need help immediately! The coordinates are attached within the carrier wave of this

missions.blitz:
Kill the Environmental Terrorists in the first pocket to unlock the gate.
Destroy the Infested Laboratory in the second pocket and warp away.
Standing:
No empire standing lost.
The Infested Laboratory is inside the Hollow Asteroid. Kill all of the Environmental Terrorists before attempting to navigate to retrieve the drop, because you do not want to be fighting them while bouncing around inside the asteroid.

missions.name: Get the Relic
missions.objective: Retrieve the Ancient Amarrian Relic from the jettisoned cargo container.
missions.briefing:
You look pretty green to me, youngster. But we've got a situation here and maybe you can help us out. 

The transport Santaros had a bulkhead failure in a nearby system and was forced to jettison its cargo. Its crew barely made it back in one piece, and the cargo's mostly a dead loss, but among the junk they had to ditch was a relic from Old Amarr, an item of little value to you or me, but vital to Lord Kendron Horowan, a noble who is friendly with House Tash-Murkon. We need someone to get out to Osoggur and find the relic. There's a reward in it for you if you succeed, as well as the gratitud

missions.name: Gone Berserk
missions.objective: Take care of the terrorist fanatics and then report back to your agent.
missions.briefing: We have reports of terrorists wreaking havoc in [system]. Our convoys have been attacked, as well as some outlying outposts; they seem to be intent on causing as much damage as possible. I'd like you to take care of them as quickly as possible. And be aware that they may not all be gathered together when you arrive... if so, you will be able to pick them off as they warp in to the rendezvous point.

pockets.name: Reinforcement 1, once for each initial group EoM Fiend

missions.blitz:
Destroy the EoM Imp from the right group, as seen from the warp-in point. Destroy its reinforcements. Get out.
Deadspace single pocket with gate.

missions.name: In The Shadow Of War
missions.objective: Rescue the stranded pilot and return him to your agent's station.
missions.briefing:
You may have been hearing some scuttlebutt about Caldari fleet movements in the area. We suspect that they're up to something big, something that might presage a general conflict. Needless to say, this constellation's been a powder keg for years, and a single spark will set the whole region alight. We're shorthanded here at [station], but we've got a number of scouts out combing the region looking for signs of enemy activity. 

About an hour ago, we received a distress call from one of our scouts in a damaged escape pod; there's a lot of subspace interference, but we were able to make out his

npcs.group: Group B (80-90km, aggro when you approach escape pod)
npcs.name: State Bushi/Dogo
Ancient Starbase Ruins
npcs.trigger: Looting the logs triggers the reinforcemnt

missions.name: Letters from Dalamaid
missions.objective: Kill the lead terrorist and recover the stolen letters of Bishop Dalamaid.
missions.briefing:
As you may have heard, the letters of Bishop Dalamaid are currently on tour through the Empire. Not only are they of doctrinal significance, but sections dealing with the various scandals of the flesh among the clergy of Dalamaid's time have always been popular. It's a little embarrassing to admit, but these tawdry details are usually the bigger draw, a fact that the Empire has rarely been too ashamed to take advantage of. This tour was one such attempt to use the controversial nature of the letters to get more citizens interested in the Scriptures. 

However, the plan has misfired. Minmatar t

pockets.name: Pocket 1
pockets.description: On arrival the Minmatar Thief is here, talks in local again, and leaves through the gate.

pockets.name: Pocket 2
pockets.description: On arrival the Minmatar Thief is here, talks in local, and leaves trough the gate.

missions.name: Lights Out
missions.objective: Kill the Gallente Lieutenant
missions.briefing:
The corporations of the State have many friends, as you know... many friends in many interesting and useful places. Why is this relevant, you ask? Well, it's not at all… it was merely an idle thought I had today that I wanted to impart upon you. 

But actually, now I come to think about the topic of friends… I am reminded of a particular friend of mine in a very difficult situation. He's due for a promotion, you see. More accurately, he would be if there weren't a few other promising individuals ahead of him in line. Such promising individuals, indeed, that I am sure their superiors would miss

npcs.name: Gallente Lieutenant [Federation Manica]
npcs.trigger: Triggere by shooting the Starbase Tower

missions.blitz: Attack the Gallente Starbase Control Tower (Large Collidable Object). Wait a few seconds. If he doesn't spawn, Destroy the starbase.

missions.name: Lost Records
missions.objective: Locate the records (1), place them in your cargo hold and get it back to me here.
missions.briefing:
Listen, the courier ship Swiftsure has just had a warp drive mishap in  and was forced to jettison its cargo lest it be damaged or lost if the core blew. They're crawling back to their home station as we speak; however, the cargo they had to ditch was a set of important Federation court records. We don't have backups available, so those records are irreplaceable, and they can't get another courier out there until tomorrow sometime. Bloody couriers. Can't get good help these days. 

, we need someone to head out there and find those records right away. There's a healthy reward in it for you if

missions.name: Lost That Bet
missions.objective: Bring Dolls (1) to your agent
missions.briefing:
The thing is, I don't know. Look... I lost a bet with this guy I know, and I was dumb enough to agree to do five forfeits-- “to be named later”-- when I thought I was going to win. Now I've got no idea what he's holding over me.

On top of that, part of the deal is that I've got to stay put, so I have to find someone who'll follow these freaking bizarre directions.

If you're willing to help me out, I'll upload the station info to your journal; all you have to do is go pick up the item that doesn't belong there and bring it back to me.
|collapsed= yes

missions.name: Mysterious Sightings
missions.objective: Look for signs of any "alien activity".
missions.briefing:
We've been getting a number of reports from in and around this system regarding "unidentified alien spacecraft." Sightings of weird-looking people have even come in from planetary surfaces. Theories abound, and people are saying it's the Jovians, or even one of the Ancients: Takhmal, Yan Jung, any number of things. A few folks even think it's some new alien race making an appearance.

Anyway, we could use someone to go and check these rumors out. Supposedly there was a sighting very recently out in . If you're ready, head on out there and see what you can find. And don't worry — you'll be well

missions.name: Onslaught
missions.objective: Make contact with a small operation that's fallen silent during a mining rights standoff.
missions.briefing:
Hey, , ever been screwed over by the big corporations? Well, here's a chance for some payback. There's controversy out in the  system, where ORE lays dubious mining claim to a massive asteroid field. Problem is, independent groups working the field are at risk of being consumed – literally. There's something strange going on out there. Interested in finding out what's happening, and striking a blow for the little guy? 

---

We don't stand much on formality here: This isn't a post people seek out to advance their careers. Most of us are content to not be noticed. People start making noise, the hard-asses start remembering we're here, and that means more work for everyone.

But speaking of work, I've got some for you. We've been getting a few reports of strange activity in this system. Now, I bet it's just smugglers. And honestly, I'd rather not create too much of a fuss over it ― but it's making the civvies nervous, and they could raise just as much hell for us as any smuggler. So, just go out to these coordinates

This, : All the great nations in the galaxy have long allowed each other to travel freely in pursuit of commerce, but I've just heard a story that makes me think this time of freedom is near its end.

Pisharpar Corilian is one of Amarr's most renowned poets of all time. He is gifted, but a typical example of a poet. He's effete, arrogant, and overly sensitive — always with his head in the clouds and his feet floating half a meter off the floor. In Corilian's case, this is literal: So obese that his legs can no longer support his weight, he uses a hoverchair to get around. 

He is also a spy. H

missions.name: Clearing a Path
missions.objective: Destroy all enemy ships
missions.briefing:
The time has come to strike a blow against the arrogant Gallente. We're going to stage a raid deep into Gallente territory. In order to mask the movements of our forces, however, we first need to wipe out their pickets and patrols. We could do this with our own ships, of course, but that might alert the enemy to our plans. A lone, talented capsuleer, on the other hand, would make short work of them and raise less suspicion. 

I've uploaded the coordinates of the stepping-off point of our raid into your nav-computer. I want you to go there and destroy all of the enemy ships you find. Be advised

missions.name: Communications Cold War
missions.objective: Destroy the Gallente communications array and the Federation pilots guarding it.
missions.briefing:
The Gallente have constructed a communications relay station very near the borders of our space. This relay is a problem because the Gallente have grown increasingly belligerent and intractable lately, and this communications relay would be able to operate outside of normal channels, enhancing the espionage capabilities of any Gallente spies that make their way into Amarr space. 

We want you to enter the  system and destroy the communications platform, along with the Federation pilots guarding it. We are expecting it to be guarded, but not heavily, because they think we know nothing of this l

Well, it just so happens that we're in the middle of some operations that might require a capsuleer's assistance. 

The Amarrians have been busy lately, building bases and moving fleet resources back and forth along the frontier. We've done recon in several of the systems where the Amarrians have been active, but we recently observed unusual comings and goings in the  system. We think they might be building a base in one of the system's asteroid fields, but we can't be sure as all of our ships are tied down in other locations. 

I'd like to install a reconnaissance module in your ship, then ha

missions.name: Drone Infestation
missions.objective: Destroy the Drone Silo and then report back to your agent.
missions.briefing: I have dire news, . One of our deadspace asteroid belts in  has been infested by rogue drones! We lost contact with the miners working there a few days ago, after they had reported sightings of mysterious craft nearby. Our reconnaissance team then confirmed our suspicions, the entire belt has been infested with those pests. Not only that but they have already erected a Drone Silo there, which the drones often use as the first building block for an actual Drone Hive. We need to act fast before they manage to grow in numbers, . Your job is to see to it that their silo is completely destroyed. I'
missions.blitz: Destroy the Drone Silo and return to agent.

missions.name: Eliminate the Pirate Campers (Guristas Pirates)
missions.objective: Eliminate the Guristas ships roaming around the stargate near the coordinates of your bookmark.
missions.briefing:
I have a job for someone of your caliber. There's a hidden stargate in  that is frequented by pilots of the Caldari State. It so happens that a gang of cretins belonging to the Guristas organization have set up camp near the stargate in the hopes of catching easy prey traveling via the gate. In other words, they will attack any passersby who aren't part of their terrorist organization. 

We've considered sending in the military, but I'm afraid the pirates might make a quick get-away should they discover who's after them. I'd like you to go there and waste them once they attack you. When you've

missions.name: Foreign Investment
missions.objective: Destroy the Federation Navy guards and their headquarters
missions.briefing:
There has been an ongoing situation in the entertainment industry, the gambling sector in particular. We've started to see Gallente companies buying out the smaller ones here and reaping profits from our own citizens. Worse, they've offered Navy vessels as security for these companies' spacebound offices, many of which are located in our own regions.

Make no mistake, : This is in all likelihood just their first step. Their intentions are nothing short of an economic war with us over one of our key industries, followed by a direct assault in our own backyard once we're sufficiently weakened.

missions.name: Freedom Must Not Prevail
missions.briefing: A few Minmatar terrorists, belonging to the despicable organization calling themselves 'The Freedom Fighters', managed to get by security and are approaching one of our slave compounds within this system. Their goal is undoubtedly to capture some of our slaves and release them onto the streets! Fortunately we spotted them before they could finish their mission. I want you to go out there and destroy them. I'll upload the coordinates of their current location into your NeoCom if you accept this mission.

missions.name: Freeing the Informant
missions.objective: Destroy the Serpentis ships; bring the informant(1) back to your agent.
missions.briefing:
As you well know, the Serpentis are notorious pirates across the galaxy. They are particularly troublesome here, pushing their drugs and committing all sorts of crimes against the Federation. Every so often, though, one of them finds a conscience and decides to change his criminal ways. 

It just so happens that a Serpentis captain is ready to turn himself in. Of course, he can't just dock in one of our stations and surrender: He'd be dead before he exited warp. Now, that wouldn't be a big deal, normally, but this one has information that can help bring down several higher ranking Serpentis me
missions.blitz: Kill The Informant, item appears in cargo, warp out.

missions.name: Gone Berserk
missions.objective: Take care of the terrorist fanatics and then report back to your agent.
missions.briefing: We have reports of terrorists wreaking havoc in . Our convoys have been attacked, as well as some outlying outposts; they seem to be intent on causing as much damage as possible. I'd like you to take care of them as quickly as possible. And be aware that they may not all be gathered together when you arrive... if so, you will be able to pick them off as they warp in to the rendezvous point.
missions.blitz:
Kill the left group of EoM ships and each subsequent spawn. Do not touch the center and right groups.
Warp to Acceleration Gate.

missions.name: Human Cattle
missions.objective: Retrieve the data chip(Encoded Data Chip - 0.1) from one of the habitation modules and return it to your agent. Please note you are not supposed to destroy the habitation module in this case.
missions.briefing:
I'd like you to help us investigate a string of disappearances which have been occurring in . The outer settlements and mining expeditions have been reporting people missing almost every day now for the past few weeks, and some small settlements have strangely become abandoned. It's become a bit of a political mess, considering the government has been encouraging civilians to move away from the cities and crowded areas, and now people are moving back to avoid the dangers of living on the outer rim of civilized space.

For your first reconnaissance mission I would like you to take a look at a s

Amarr Navy Delta I/II Support Frigate

missions.objective: Intercept the transport ships and retrieve 40x Confiscated Viral Agent (20).
missions.briefing:
Our spies discovered that a few convoys of Amarrian ships carrying a viral agent are heading to one of the planets in , with the intention of carrying out a biological attack on the populace! They must not be allowed to release the toxin into the planet's atmosphere! I desperately need you to find and intercept the convoys before they finish their mission. I can supply you with bookmarks to their last sighting, I just hope you can find them in time.

{{Co|#DB170B|Be careful though, we suspect that they have an interceptor or two guarding the convoys as well as other nasties. But I'm sure that'

missions.name: Kicking the Nest
missions.objective: Destroy Drone Hive
missions.briefing:
Yes, I have something for you.

Drones going rogue? How does this even happen? They've been stealing parts from our long-range survey towers for a while. Now they are attacking our survey ships. This has to stop. Based on data from numerous sources, we've found a gathering of these drones. A nest, if you will. Destroy them.

missions.objective: Destroy Rogue Drones
missions.briefing:
Sure, I've got something.

Our ships are armed so unless it's a serious threat, they should be able to handle most problems. Yet we've lost another one in the area. We've narrowed it down to a listening post nearby. According to their latest reports, logistics say the post had been requesting more replacement parts than usual. Make of that what you will. Good luck.

missions.name: Minmatar Plot
missions.objective: Retrieve the slaves
missions.blitz: Bypass the first pocket and just run to the 2nd acceleration gate, kill the destroyer to get the mission objective.

missions.name: Mission of Mercy
missions.objective: Secure the Medical Drone and ensure the safe arrival of the repair teams.
missions.briefing:
? Good. I have a job that's probably outside your normal scope. This one is humanitarian. 

A pandemic has broken out on a planet in the  system. Fortunately, a cure was already developed and mass-produced. The contagion originated on another planet nearby, you see, so the people of  already have experience in dealing with this particular disease. 

The Sisters of Eve sent a medical drone with the vaccine onboard down to the planet, but some degenerate pirates have intercepted the craft, disabled it, and are now trying to demand a ransom for its release. The Sisters say that if the drone is de
missions.blitz: Kill only the Lieutenants of the last wave. Note there can be Lieutenants in the 2nd wave too.

missions.name: Murderer Brought To Justice
missions.objective: Find and destroy the murderer and his guards, then report back to your agent.
missions.briefing: I just received reports of criminal activity in our sector. A certain pilot attacked and destroyed a ship belonging to an ally of ours which was flying through CONCORD-protected space. He managed to flee before CONCORD got a hold of him. I want you to fly to the coordinates where he was last spotted, find him, and put him out of business. He's an extremely elusive fellow, who has managed to evade CONCORD for quite some time, so be wary.
missions.blitz: not possible.

missions.name: No Honor
missions.objective: Destroy enemy ship; return stolen cargo(80) to agent.
missions.briefing:
I wish everyone was as straightforward as you, . Then I wouldn't be in this situation. 

I supplied this Gallente leech with certain items he requested, and then he warps away before paying me. Some people, I tell you. So, I need to teach him some manners. Well, okay… I need you to teach him some manners. 

My sources tell me he can be found at . , I'd like you to do that — teach him his manners — by flying out there, blasting his ship to so much dust, then returning my stolen cargo to me. If that doesn't teach him, nothing will.
missions.blitz: Destroy the hauler, "Stolen goods" will automagically appear in your cargo, and warp off.

missions.name: Opening Moves
missions.briefing:
Oh, we have a number of possibilities, but only a few really choice assignments for especially trustworthy capsuleers like yourself. 

These are definitely dangerous times, . The corsairs are getting bolder, crime is rampant, and the empires are pretty much at war, which will plunge us all into chaos and cost millions of lives. Unfortunately for people like me, there's not much we can do to change the inevitable; all we can do is try to make sure that what comes costs us as little as possible — in lives, worlds, and materials. To that end, I'm overseeing a lot of war prep in this region, and I

missions.briefing: A convoy was spotted in a nearby system, apparently en route straight through T22-QI. This is a perfect chance for you to show us what you're worth. This convoy comes with a medium-sized escort, nothing you shouldn't be able to handle. I want you to intercept the convoy after it makes the jump into T22-QI; Destroy the ships if they don't surrender their goods, and bring anything you can salvage back to me. I'll split the loot. Sounds fair eh?

missions.objective: Eliminate the UDI reinforcements, then report back to your agent.
missions.briefing:
Recent reports from the Federal Intelligence Office give us reason to believe that the UDI terrorist organization has begun setting up outposts in the names of front corporations at deadspace forward points within our sovereign space, with the intention of spying on Gallente corporations. We recently managed to confirm the existence of one such outpost in this system. 

Operatives have already been sent in to cripple key outpost structures, and the UDI's response has been to call in a great deal of mercenary reinforcements, with our intel telling us more are on the way. We believe they may be

missions.name: Rogue Drone Harassment
missions.objective: Destroy all Rogue Drones
missions.briefing: We've just been informed of an official complaint from the mining division working in one of the asteroid fields in , about their haulers being attacked on sight by roaming rogue drones in the area. At first it started with just a few unsuccessful attacks, but the situation has worsened greatly and the hauler pilots, along with the miners themselves, are on the verge of a strike. This is the perfect opportunity for someone like you to take care of business and earn your reward, . I'll supply you with a bookmark to the location of the asteroid belt, eliminate the rogue drone threat there and th

missions.name: Save a Man's Career
missions.objective: Find Sangrel Minn, destroy his ship and loot the reports (0.1).
missions.briefing: Recently a thief stole some valuable documents from a high ranking officer and friend of mine in . These documents are very sensitive and we fear they were stolen by someone working for an enemy of ours. Fortunately, the information stored within the documents was encrypted using the very latest encryption software. The thief probably did not expect this, and we believe the information has not yet been revealed to him or his employer. Should it leak out however then the leader of  will be ... less than forgiving. Would you please save my friends career? I have been told by a reliable source of

missions.objective: Find the former secret agent and eliminate him before he can reveal the top-secret information to agents from DED.
missions.briefing:
Yes, I do and I think this is something you'll love .

We have a bit of a problem on our hands with a recently fired employee of ours. He was working as a deep cover agent for us, and now he is threatening to release all the information to DED about what we have been doing. We cannot have this happen. If he does make it to the DED headquarters in , Impetus will face serious repercussions. Eliminate this threat as soon as possible to ensure that he doesn't get the information to DED agents. I'll put into your way-points where he was last seen. Return here and let me know that he is dead.

missions.name: Small Misunderstanding
missions.objective: Destroy the damaged Caldari ship.
missions.briefing:
I do, in fact, have a little matter that needs your attention. It's rather... delicate. 

My corporation recently had a small misunderstanding with a Caldari business rival. Fortunately, after some of our folks met with the Caldari to have a… discussion, our people managed to limp back here; unfortunately, they left the wreckage of the Caldari ship intact, including the ship's log. 

Now, it would impact any future dealings we might have with the Caldari if this dispute became known to their authorities. I'm sure they'd insist on petty things like vengeance. So we'd rather that didn't happen.

This mission requires a Data Analyzer.

missions.briefing: Agents working for the Cartel recently managed to infiltrate one of our outposts in a nearby system. We have reliable information that they did manage to find classified information about our various operations in the area. Fortunately one of the officers at the outpost managed to slip a tracking device into one of their ships before they escaped. We know they are located in  at this very moment. I would like you to fly over there and ambush them immediately. Return to me once their ships have been turned to dust. But be careful, they are armed and dangerous.

Pirates tend to create these temporary blockades at ill-defended stargates, usually ones which have been set up by civilians and are not guarded by our forces. But we still have an obligation to protect the citizens of . Therefore I want you, , to fly to the stargate and eliminate the illegal blockade.

This particular traitor is fairly high-profile, you see, which makes it difficult for us to act more openly. While this is a bit of a concern in and of itself, the bigger problem is that we believe the cartel is being paid by elements within the Federation intelligence community; their goal, apparently, is to use this particular drug route as a way to insert intelligence assets into the State. That is of considerably greater concern to us.

We would like you to fly to the coordinates of this rendezvous, eliminate the Serpentis vessels there, and

missions.briefing:
Well, we have a bit of a problem we need to deal with, . A low-level executive here has consistently failed to live up to our standards, but because of his position in a sensitive area of the corporation, simply terminating his contract would have an unfortunate effect on our stock prices. As a result, it has become necessary to— shall we say, "eliminate his salary line"? Yes, that works. Eliminate his salary line, through alternate means. 

This executive and his entourage, consisting of a number of security vessels, will be stopping by a transfer station in the  system shortly. We would like

We've spotted an unauthorized Ammatar personnel transport flying through . Cargo scanners revealed a load of militants on board, no doubt a part of a ground force intended to strike a target near here. We don't know where they are headed or what they're up to, but we definitely want to find out. I request that you intercept the transport and capture the men on board. Bring them back to us for questioning.

We believe the escort team is split up into a few groups. If you strike fast and hard, you'll probably avoid getting swarmed by them all at once. Just keep a look out for any stray ships; we

Fortunately, we've just gotten intelligence regarding the lunatic's current whereabouts. Head out there, will you? Stop this madman from executing his fanatic scheme.

missions.briefing:
Even in times of conflict with an external enemy we still find ourselves in competition with other “friendly” corporations. One such corporation is providing security for a repair outpost. They got that security contract by underbidding us. Go to the site, destroy their entire security force, then destroy the repair outpost. With such an abysmal failure on their record, they'll never get another security contract in this sector again. 

And who knows, maybe we'll get the reconstruction contract for the replacement outpost as well.

f you're willing. Given the current state of interstellar affairs, Mordu's Legion is in the thick of things. They've been engaging in raids and reconnaissance, harassing merchant traffic, and generally making trouble for us. In this sector, at least, they're doing just what the Caldari want them to — keeping our fleet tied down so those greedy bastards can martial their own forces in relative peace. 

We might have an ace in the hole, however. We have good intelligence that many Intaki inside the Caldari State are growing impatient and restless, suffering discrimination and unemployment. Many

missions.objective: Find the Blood Raider slavers and destroy them.
missions.briefing:
Now that we've destroyed the slave pen and rescued the captives, it's time to turn our attention to the slavers themselves. You have the full authority of the government to shoot to kill, we do not expect any prisoners. And please do not confuse these low-lifes to the legit slave-users we sometimes do business with. These Blood Raider fanatics kidnap people indiscriminately, instead of only targeting criminals and POWs like the Amarr Empire.

We believe we know of the current whereabouts of these slavers. Warp to the bookmark that I will upload into your NeoCom, and try to find them somewhere
missions.blitz: In the last pocket you only have to kill the Slavers-- this completes the mission and you can warp out afterwards. All other kills are optional.

missions.briefing:
This one should be pretty simple. Near here is a mining colony owned and run by some Mannar workers. Usually, they keep things pretty solid. We even have a deal with them where we protect them and supply them with parts in exchange for a cheap supply of minerals. It all works out fine. 

Recently, though, they've had some problems with rogue drones, and some of their equipment was damaged. Trouble is, we're in the middle of arming to protect against the Caldari threat, so we can't wait for them to fix their machines. Instead, I've been authorized to have their tools repaired.

I need you to destroy the monument, and any witnesses to the event. This may include some of our own people, but you needn't worry about any kind of standings complications, I will handle the political fallout.

We recently managed to confirm the existence of one such outpost in this system.

missions.briefing: I just received an alert across multiple communication channels asking for evacuation help. A diplomatic station not far from here is undergoing a traumatic systems shut-down cascade. It's all going, and people need off that station. The rescue requires a smaller ship than what they have available to get in past the field of debris now ringing the station. That's what happens when none of the empires will trust the others enough to allow them to send in their best station engineers and construction teams, and so they all agree on a third-party freelance company to build their stations. Happens

The only information we have is a garbled message. Listen... 

...*SQUAWK crackle crackle* ... unknown intruders ... picking apart ... communications ... *hisssss* ... help us! ... escorts are ..."

We fear its already too late, but we have to do something. We‘ve made

missions.briefing:
Yes, that's true. You're , right? So many new capsuleers these days – hard to keep them all straight. My apologies. 

Anyway, listen: We recently lost contact with a small group of miners at . Outside of the miners' distress beacon being activated, we've heard nothing from them for two days. Just... silence. 

Normally, we'd just hire new crews. But we spent a fortune on implants for those miners, and we're not ready to write those off. So would you please go to  and see what's what? 

Whatever the case, you'll need to bring those miners back here for examination and questioning.

Now to cut the story short, what I need is a ship destroyed. But there is a 'slight' problem, which I'll get to. You see, this ship isn't just any old pirate ship, it contains a high ranking official within Hyasyoda Corporation, who also happens to be a member within the Caldari State government. This isn't a rescue mission, this is a destroy mission. But don't worry, this mission is completely confidential. If you're interested, the

Fortunately one of the officers at the outpost managed to slip a tracking device into one of their ships before they escaped. We know they are located in  at this very moment. I would like you to fly over there and ambush them immediately. Return to me once their ships have been turned to dust. But be careful, they are armed and dangerous.

It is a privately owned trading outpost which is renowned for attracting criminal elements, and I wouldn't be the least bit surprised that such activity is going on there.

We believe the goods are being transported to the outpost as we speak, in an industrial, a Bestower to be exact. I want you to fly out there immediately and find the equipment and bring it back to us. It may still be located in the same sealed containers as when it was stole
missions.blitz: Kill all enemy ships in Pocket 1. Destroying the Docked Bestower in Pocket 2 and retrieving the content will complete the mission. The Seven have showed their ugly faces again, in . They've set up a prison facility where they keep captured personnel working for Caldari State/Minmatar Republic and random civilians for a ransom. The people that are put in there are rarely the same after they've been released. The Seven have somehow managed to get a hold on Sansha mind altering gadgets, which they use on those who they kidnap to erase their memory among other things. Rarely does this not have serious side effects ...

I want that place shut down, immediately. Do whatever it takes.

This guy has a very long history of criminal and terrorist activity, belonging to the international network of thugs calling themselves the Freedom Fighters. I want his head on a platter, and the slaves back here alive.

After countless hours of investigation, our agents have finally discovered their primary armory facility. This is a storage facility for their ship modules and weaponry for ground troops, and would explain how quickly their lost equipment is replaced in .

They have apparently brought with them a small group of highly skilled Minmatar Fleet pilots, who apparently will defend them with their lives. We can't afford to lose any more of our ships, so I ask you to do us a favor and eliminate the escort ships guarding the miners.

We don't care about the Gallente civilians, but we do care about the massive amount of sensitive information that might be stored in the remnants of the outpost's central information core. That outpost was a communications hub routing vast amounts of data back into Gallente space. There's no telling what secrets we could uncover with that information core. 

We want you to go to the remnant

Have you ever heard of Anire Scarlet? Well, she's practically an urban legend among the local pirates. It seems any outlaw with a clone bay and rhetorical skills can amass a following these days. A couple of extremely dangerous Blood Raider battleships just ambushed a heavily armed convoy of ours, wiping out the entire escort in moments. The survivors were captured, and God knows what they did to them. The Raiders managed to fight off the Amarr Empire officers who arrived at the scene, but we managed to trace them to . I want you to go out there and destroy them, and then bring back the goods they looted from the convoy. I'll upload the approximate coordinates to your NeoCom if you accept this job.

missions.briefing:
There's any number of memorial sites from the Great War between us and the Caldari littered around our space. We've always honored the wishes of honest Caldari pilgrims wanting to visit these memorials; we even let them keep a small number of maintenance staff around on the bigger sites. 

It seems our trust in them was misplaced, however. I've recently received reports of their using their memorials, of all places, as staging grounds for production facilities inside our own space. Those profit-minded slime are seeking to benefit financially from these facilities, all without paying taxes… in

A hand-written, hand-delivered letter — on actual paper! — arrived in the office this morning, expressing Admiral Meledier's desire to defect. Having a high-ranking Gallente officer suddenly join our side could cause tremendous damage to the ability of their capsuleers to wage war against us: His knowledge of their systems and tactics would be staggering. 

We want you to go collect him from the rendezvous point in . You game?

A person whose job it is to spread good news and make sure the general public doesn't lose faith in the government. Yes, I guess you could call it "propaganda management"… but hell, what's a government without propaganda?

missions.briefing:
We have a lead on a potential slave trade in Adiere and I need someone to patrol that area and find out what's happening. missions.blitz:
In the first pocket, use the following kill order:
4x Battlecruisers
2x Elite Frigates
3x Elite Cruisers
2x Elite Frigates
It is necessary to one-shot each of the elite frigates in Group 3 in order to avoid immediately spawning reinforcements. The acceleration will be unlocked as long as there are no remaining hostile targets. If reinforcements spawn (whether due to not one-shotting the frigates, or taking too long to activate the gate), the gate will be locked until all hostile targets are destroyed.
In the second pocket, approach the . The mission is flagged as completed once the first three spawns have been destroyed.

They are using some sort of scrambling device to deactivate our sentry guns as well. We request that you gather a group of battle-hardened pilots and take them on. If you don't, then I'm afraid we'll have to negotiate our way out of this ... or just keep our fingers crossed that other backup arrives in time.
missions.blitz:

Warning: It can be helpful to fit a  or  to help counteract sensor dampening.

missions.briefing: Pirates have set up a blockade near a hidden stargate in Ivar, and are swarming over any passersby, which are mostly merchants and civilians. Pirates tend to create these temporary blockades at ill-defended stargates, usually ones which have been set up by civilians and are not guarded by our forces. But we still have an obligation to protect the citizens of Odengsolf. Therefore I want you, , to fly to the stargate and eliminate the illegal blockade.

He talked openly about his plan without a second thought. We need your help in stopping him before he can carry out his plans.

These pirates have crossed our path before, and are not to be taken lightly. They belong to an organization which split itself from the Serpentis many years ago, and has grown quite large in a surprisingly short amount of time. Their leader uses the nickname 'Zazzmatazz', which you might have heard of if you're into Gallente films.

Anyway, the convoy escort drove off the pirate attack and

missions.name: The Rogue Slave Trader (Sansha's Nation) (1 of 2)
missions.wikiTitle: The Rogue Slave Trader (Sansha's Nation) (1 of 2)
missions.objective: Rescue 50x prisoners (375 m3)
missions.briefing:
We currently have a rogue slave trader on our hands,  The Sansha's Nation has apparently dispatched a group of their own to  to look for potential victims. They've been raiding outlying settlements, which have weak security systems in place.

Our primary objective right now is to get back the people they've taken captive. We think we've located their slave pen in , but it's your job to make sure. Destroy the structure and rescue the slaves if you can, then report back to me. We'll deal with these slave traders later.
missions.blitz:
Warp in to preferred distance. Kill or ignore the initial group and destroy the slave pen. Pickup the prisoners and warp out.
Single ungated pocket. The prisoners drop from the slave pen. An additional Sansha Slaver battleship will spawn (near the Beacon) 8 minutes after warp-in.
Don't forget to destroy the slave pen (40km) and pickup the prisoners, you may want to fit an afterburner or use a tractor beam.
When the spawn [finally] arrives he will attack the Noctis salvaging, be prepared or just don't bother waiting for it.

A corporate executive seems to have used some rather unorthodox methods to try to speed up production in his mining fields, and the strain this placed on the labor force there has led to a wildcat strike which has shut down production in that field entirely, causing materials shortages all the way up our supply chain. Of course, this is entirely unacceptable; it is imperative that this strike be brought to a close immediately and production resume as soon as possible.

I want you to head out there and destroy the raiders' base, along with every damned Minmatar you find. If you're quick and decisive, you can take these worthless dogs down.

pockets.description: Everything aggros on warp-in. Make sure to destroy the Remote Cloaking Array while you're here. This pocket contains an acceleration gate back to the first pocket.

pockets.name: Pocket 3
pockets.description: Again, everything aggros on warp-in. Make sure to destroy the Remote Cloaking Array while you're here. This pocket also contains an acceleration gate back to the first pocket.

pockets.name: Pocket 4
pockets.description: Everything aggros on warp-in. This pocket contains an acceleration gate back to the first pocket. If you destroyed the Remote Cloaking Arrays in pockets 2 and 3, then there will be another acceleration gate that will take you to pocket 5.

pockets.name: Pocket 5
pockets.description: Everything aggros on warp-in. This pocket contains an acceleration gate back to the first pocket as well as an acceleration gate to pocket 6.

pockets.name: Pocket 6
pockets.description: Everything aggros on warp-in. This pocket contains the Minmatar Station which you must destroy to complete the mission.

A couple of years ago, the Serpentis Corporation built a station out in Sakulda and everyone in the constellation has been worried about it since. At first, as always with the Serpentis, things were fine -  they kept us a clean front and acted like a legitimate corporation. More recently, though, they've been pirating nearby gates and causing all kinds of problems for the local police forces. missions.name: Rogue Eradication
missions.briefing:
Well, we nearly succeeded in wiping out all rogue drones who knew about the research Ashur Ylavun had stolen, but it seems two drones escaped and thus were able to share the data with the local “hive mind” in two other locations in the  system. We have enough ships to cordon off the area, and we've set up temporary jump gates between the locations for you, but we need you to actually go in there and clean the drones out. We must be sure to destroy them all before they can send their data to yet another hive. 

Don´t take any chances; these locations will be well defended.

missions.name: Sansha Acquisition
missions.briefing:
I do, yes. It's quite sensitive, actually, so I'd appreciate it if this matter remained between us. It seems some cybernetics experts among the Caldari and the god-fearing Amarr have recently made efforts to acquire Sansha mindslave technology. Now, we don't know what they're after, specifically, but we can't afford to let them jump ahead of us. We have a couple of sympathizers among them who we think we can convince to defect, so maybe they can keep us up to date on what has transpired with this Sansha research.

missions.name: The Fortress (Minmatar Republic)
missions.briefing:
“All work and no play,” you know. But I can see that you're determined. Yes, well, there is something I had planned to hand over to the authorities, but maybe you can handle this one without us having to get them involved. In fact, that might be best, now that I think about it. 

, these Minmatar rebels, as you know, are always causing problems, so their raids in outlying systems don't normally draw too much notice. Not normally. Recently, though, we've been hearing rumors about some kind of “super” battle station they're building in Tamo, supposedly with a full capital ship maintenance array.
